De sluis in Aadorp is het coördinatiecen:
The lock at Aadorp is the coordination centre for the canal. You must report to this lock when you wish to enter the canal. Inland vessels must report for registration in IVS90, the reporting and tracking system for inland shipping. If you are coming from the direction of Coevorden, your expected arrival time will be passed on by the bridge keeper of the Moniersbrug to the lock keeper of the Coevordersluis. During busy periods in summer you may experience delays. We do our best to operate the Coevordersluis at least once an hour.
•	Additional operation for commercial shipping only, on request (requests at least 24 hours in advance to the lock at Aadorp), telephone 038 499 70 40
•	To lock through you must be at the lock 15 minutes before closing time, otherwise you can no longer take part in the locking process
•	The bridges operated by the province of Overijssel are not operated from wind force 7 Beaufort upwards.

Spertijden zijn van vijf minuten voor to:
Closure periods run from five minutes before to five minutes past the hour and from five minutes before to five minutes past the half hour. (So XX:55 to XX:05 and XX:25 to XX:35.)

Monday to Friday the closure periods start at 05:55 – 06:05 and the last closure period is 00:25 – 00:35 the following day.

On Saturday the closure periods start at 06:55 – 07:05 and the last closure period is 19:55 – 20:05.

On Sunday the closure periods start at 08:55 – 09:05. From 1 April to 31 October the last closure period is 18:55 – 19:05, and from 1 November to 31 March the last closure period is 16:55-17:05.

Up to 10 minutes before the hour and half hour a 5-minute opening may still take place.

If operation on request is needed on Saturday or Sunday after the opening hours, the weekday closure periods apply.

Als gevolg van de slechte staat waarin d:
Owing to the poor condition of the bridge, it was necessary for road-traffic safety to strengthen the existing bridge deck. A supporting structure has been fitted beneath the existing deck so that its load-bearing capacity for road traffic is amply sufficient. As a result the bridge can no longer be opened, and in addition the air draught has been reduced from 1.70 m to 0.90 m.

The council of the municipality of Steenwijkerland has yet to take a final decision on the next steps. The measures taken therefore apply for an indefinite period.
(email from the Municipality of Steenwijkerland, 29-06-2007)

Voorrangsregeling:\nBij doorvaart van de:
Priority arrangement:
When passing through the lock, commercial shipping may, in accordance with established guidelines, be given priority over recreational craft.
 
The main provisions of this priority arrangement are:
1. On grounds of safety and the smooth flow of shipping, the Plassenschap may grant the right of priority for locking through the Mijndense Sluis to:
a. vessels with a length of 20m or more, which also
b. fulfil an economic function.
2. The right of priority applies only during the period from 1 May up to and including 15 September.
3. Vessels wishing to use the granted right of priority must, when approaching the lock, fly the red pennant at such a height that it is clearly visible.
1.\tHoudt rekening met een wachttijd van:
1.	Allow for a waiting time of two hours.

2.	The block regime starts each morning on the north side.
     
3.	No passage for commercial shipping through the Jachtensluis.

4.	Recreational craft with a mast higher than 18.60 m may, with the lock keeper's permission, be locked through the eastern lock chamber for commercial shipping (lock chamber with bascule bridge). Other recreational craft are not permitted to be locked through the lock chambers for commercial shipping.

5.	In the event of an incident, or owing to special circumstances, the lock keeper may deviate from this and allow locking through one of the three commercial-shipping chambers. This is communicated on VHF channel VHF 18.

Boating season from 1 April to 1 October, between sunrise and sunset.

Maximum speed for all vessels (incl. speedboats): 9 km/h.

Maximum draught: 0.50 metres.

Vessels must meet the following requirements:
the maximum permitted length is 12.50 metres;
the maximum permitted beam is 2.75 metres;
navigation is allowed between sunrise and sunset.

Motorised water sports (such as jet skis, waterskiing and flyboarding) not permitted
Lowest bridge: 1.98 metres (Voorstraatbrug Hardenberg).
Bediening vindt plaats op het halve uur:
Operation takes place on the half hour between 06.00 – 22.00

No operation for recreational craft from 06:30-09:30 and from 15:30-18:30.

Last opening for recreational craft is at 06:15 in the morning and at 15:15 in the afternoon.

If an opening takes place for sea-going vessels or other high, commercially 
operated vessels, recreational craft may make use of that opening. 

The bridge operator may deviate from the operating times  in connection with safety/incidents on the road and the water.

Outside the visitor season the bridges open only on request, for winter berth-holders, permanent berth-holders and events.
